key_insight: An automated seam welding system with controlled rotation, rigid clamping, and cooled electrodes can produce more uniform pipe welds while sharply reducing leakage failures.
engineering_relevance: The design demonstrates how mechanical automation and process control can improve productivity, safety, and quality in cylindrical pipe welding.
method: Built a robust frame with workpiece fixtures and pneumatic clamping for vibration-free alignment.
Used a gear motor to rotate pipes at 10–15 RPM for continuous seam welding.
Integrated internal water-cooled CuCrZr rotating wheel electrodes to limit heat buildup and degradation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a comparative evaluation of CRCS and ER34 pipes using 11–13 A welding current and 2.5 mm wall thickness, with leakage failures reduced from 15% to below 3%.
watch_item: Performance may depend on tight control of current, rotation speed, clamping rigidity, and cooling, so scaling to other pipe sizes or materials may require re-optimization.
paper_title: Design and Development of Automatic Seam Welding of Pipes
